reasoning: France sit atop the outright market after a convincing opener, with the deepest forward line in the field and Mbappe as the player most likely to win a knockout tie alone, while Spain remain co-favorites despite a flat scoreless draw with Cape Verde and key midfield injuries to Fermin Lopez and doubts over Merino. England (Kane in form after a 4-2 win over Croatia) plus reigning champions Argentina and five-time winners Brazil headline the chasing pack, so the title race remains tightly bunched this early. I lean France for their balance of squad depth, knockout pedigree under Deschamps and a manageable remaining group, with Spain as runner-up given their elite young core.
